_meta.source_note: Seeded from NVD, CISA KEV, and vendor advisories through mid-2025. Validate against live feeds before publishing.

vendors.name: CrowdStrike
vendors.wr_take: Best patch velocity in endpoint. July 2024 was an ops failure not a breach — but 8.5M machines proved dangerous concentration.

cve_list.description: Falcon sensor privilege escalation via kernel driver race condition
Falcon console stored XSS via malformed alert description field
Falcon Data Replicator S3 path traversal — read-only adjacent tenant data

ai_era_grade.reasoning: CrowdStrike demonstrates published AI usage (Charlotte AI agentic analyst, language models for data classification) but provides insufficient technical detail on autonomous remediation capabilities, zero evidence of AI workload defense, and no disclosure of AI governance, guardrails, or adversarial AI readiness.

cve_list.description: Agent local privilege escalation on Windows via unquoted service path
Management console SSRF via webhook configuration endpoint

ai_era_grade.reasoning: SentinelOne demonstrates autonomous response capabilities and some AI-era awareness via claims of defending against AI model-based supply chain attacks, but evidence is limited to self-published marketing claims without independent validation, velocity benchmarks, or transparency on internal AI controls.

vendors.name: Ivanti
vendors.wr_take: Remediation pace is structural not situational. 16 active KEVs is not bad luck — it is product lifecycle debt that marketing cannot patch.

cve_list.description: Connect Secure stack buffer overflow — zero-day exploited by UNC5337 (PRC) before disclosure
Connect Secure local privilege escalation chained with CVE-2025-0282
EPMM command injection — required emergency repatch after incomplete initial fix

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Ivanti demonstrates scoped autonomy in endpoint management through self-healing workflows and autonomous patching capabilities, but lacks published velocity benchmarks, evidence of AI workload defense coverage, internal AI governance practices, and adversarial AI readiness posture.

vendors.name: Microsoft Defender
vendors.wr_take: January 2024 Midnight Blizzard breach: password spray on a legacy test tenant, found by incident response not alerting. Security team email accessed for over a month.

cve_list.description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint spoofing via crafted file
Windows TCP/IP RCE exploitable without authentication

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Single marketing-focused source mentions 'AI agents in security' and 'automation at machine speed' but provides no technical specifications for scope, controls, or AI attack surface capabilities; insufficient evidence to substantiate higher autonomy scores or any broader AI readiness claims.

vendors.name: Palo Alto Networks
vendors.wr_take: CVE-2024-3400 was actively exploited before public disclosure, suggesting detection lagged threat actor activity. Patch cadence and transparency improved, but reactive posture on zero-days remains exposed.

cve_list.description: PAN-OS auth bypass in management interface — exploited in the wild pre-patch
GlobalProtect gateway RCE via malformed handshake packet
Panorama SQL injection via device group filter parameter

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Palo Alto demonstrates scoped automation (inline deep learning protection in Nebula) and strategic awareness of agentic security gaps through the Koi acquisition announcement, but the evidence bundle consists entirely of marketing material and acquisition announcements lacking technical specificity, published benchmarks, third-party validation, or details on internal AI governance.

vendors.name: Fortinet
vendors.wr_take: FortiOS has been a persistent KEV fixture for three years. At some point this stops being bad luck and starts being architecture.

cve_list.description: FortiOS SSL-VPN out-of-bounds write — unauthenticated RCE exploited by multiple threat actors
FortiOS format string vuln in fgfmd daemon — unauthenticated RCE
FortiAP stack-based buffer overflow in CLI command handler

ai_era_grade.reasoning: FortiAI claims to automate security operations, but single evidence source uses only vague marketing language without published benchmarks, specific threat coverage, or vendor AI posture details needed to score across four dimensions.

vendors.name: Cisco
vendors.wr_take: October 2024 DevHub breach exposed GitHub tokens and hardcoded credentials. Investigation showed misconfiguration not attack — but that is the definition of detection lag.

cve_list.description: NX-OS CLI command injection — root RCE exploited by Velvet Ant (PRC-nexus)
ASA/FTD management tunnel DoS — part of ArcaneDoor PRC campaign
ASA persistent backdoor via crafted VPN client package

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Single blog post excerpt provides only high-level product positioning ('understand and mitigate risk') without specific technical features, benchmarks, or vendor practices needed to assess AI-Era Readiness.

vendors.name: Check Point
vendors.wr_take: October 2024 incident handled with minimal public detail. The choice to limit scope of disclosure is itself a data point.

cve_list.description: Security Gateway path traversal enabling credential extraction — exploited ITW
Quantum Gateway local privilege escalation via kernel module

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected for Check Point's AI-Era Readiness posture; all dimensions scored NA per rubric protocol.

vendors.name: Juniper Networks
vendors.wr_take: Five critical RCE flaws disclosed together in early 2024, then a vendor communication error came to light when WatchTowr highlighted the messaging gap. Structural disclosure process issue, not transient.

cve_list.description: Junos OS SRX/EX unauthenticated RCE via J-Web — exploited ITW
Junos OS Routing Protocol Daemon DoS via malformed BGP update

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected; no public documentation, blog posts, or product materials found to assess any dimension.

vendors.name: Citrix
vendors.wr_take: CitrixBleed was exploited in the wild since August 2023 before the October patch. Customers are still managing fallout two years later.

cve_list.description: NetScaler ADC/Gateway unauthenticated RCE — mass exploitation by ransomware groups
Virtual Apps and Desktops privilege escalation via session recording component

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected; no public documentation found to assess Citrix's AI-era readiness across any dimension.

cve_list.description: CloudFormation SSRF via template URL parameter allowing metadata access

ai_era_grade.reasoning: AWS claims agentic AI capabilities in WorkSpaces and OpenSearch, but evidence is limited to marketing titles without substantive product details—no benchmarks, AI attack surface coverage, own-AI governance, or adversarial readiness data provided.

vendors.name: Microsoft Azure
vendors.wr_take: Storm-0558 used a 2021 signing key to forge auth tokens in July 2023. Disclosure was clear; the CSRB report on key lifecycle governance was less so.

cve_list.description: Windows Update rollback enabling reintroduction of patched flaws — exploited ITW
Hyper-V elevation of privilege — zero-day exploited before Patch Tuesday
DWM Core Library privilege escalation used in QakBot post-exploitation

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Azure's AI Anomaly Detector is a general-purpose time-series detection service with no evidence of autonomous remediation, security-focused AI workload defense, published internal AI controls, or adversarial AI readiness; single marketing page provides insufficient detail for scoring velocity.

vendors.name: Broadcom / VMware
vendors.wr_take: Post-acquisition patching velocity has degraded. vCenter and ESXi remain tier-1 ransomware targets. The Broadcom integration has not helped.

cve_list.description: vCenter Server heap overflow in DCERPC — unauthenticated RCE
vCenter privilege escalation to root chained with CVE-2024-38812
Workstation/Fusion XHCI USB use-after-free enabling VM escape

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No public evidence found for any dimension; all scored NA per rubric guidelines (do not guess).

vendors.name: Okta
vendors.wr_take: 2023 breach found via customer report not internal detection — but disclosure and recovery were textbook. RRR tells the nuanced story.

cve_list.description: Classic Engine session token fixation allowing account takeover without MFA
On-prem AD agent authentication bypass — legacy agent versions only

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Single evidence source contains only podcast title and navigation text; lacks substantive product claims, technical features, published benchmarks, policies, or audit evidence required to assess any AI-Era Readiness dimension.

vendors.name: Microsoft Entra ID
vendors.wr_take: CVE-2025-55241 reported July 14, patched July 23, no wild exploitation. Cross-tenant admin takeover potential treated with textbook urgency and transparency.

cve_list.description: SharePoint deserialization RCE with Entra auth bypass chain

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected; cannot score without product documentation, benchmarks, or published security claims.

cve_list.description: Privilege Cloud session token insufficient validation allowing privilege escalation

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected; cannot score without product documentation, public benchmarks, or feature disclosures.

vendors.name: BeyondTrust
vendors.wr_take: December 2024 breach via compromised API key hit US Treasury. Detection was external. The PAM vendor getting PAMed is a reputational category event.

cve_list.description: Privileged Remote Access command injection — exploited in Treasury breach Dec 2024
Password Safe OS command injection requiring existing admin credentials

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No public evidence collected for BeyondTrust; insufficient documentation to assess AI-Era Readiness across any dimension.

cve_list.description: Enterprise path traversal on Windows — unauthenticated arbitrary file read
Enterprise RCE via insecure session storage deserialization — requires auth

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected; cannot score any dimension without public documentation, product feature details, or published benchmarks.

vendors.name: SolarWinds
vendors.wr_take: Meaningful improvement since SUNBURST. Faster patches, better disclosure. Still carries institutional trust debt procurement teams have not fully priced in.

cve_list.description: Web Help Desk Java deserialization RCE — unauthenticated, exploited ITW
Web Help Desk hardcoded credential allowing unauthorized database access

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Evidence bundle contains only generic blog posts about AI trends in tech and social media with zero substantive information about SolarWinds product capabilities, autonomous remediation, attack surface coverage, internal AI governance, or adversarial defense research.

vendors.name: Tenable
vendors.wr_take: September 2025 Salesforce breach exposed support ticket subject lines only. Core vulnerability assessment platform never touched. Isolation worked.

cve_list.description: Nessus path traversal allowing authenticated read of arbitrary server files

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Tenable demonstrates scoped autonomous capabilities (Hexa AI configures scans for supply chain threats in minutes) and prompt injection detection on AI model endpoints, but lacks evidence of broad autonomous remediation, sub-minute detection velocity, comprehensive AI workload defense beyond prompt injection, published internal AI governance, or adversarial research on agentic attackers.

cve_list.description: InsightVM stored XSS via asset tag field allowing session hijack

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Rapid7 acknowledges AI in security operations and conducts research on AI security risks (MCP, prompt injection), but the evidence is primarily blog content and thought leadership without specific product feature claims, published benchmarks, or third-party verification.

vendors.name: Cloudflare
vendors.wr_take: Detected the Okta-linked intrusion in 23 minutes, self-contained it, then published one of the most detailed public incident reports ever written. This is what React A looks like.

cve_list.description: Workers sandbox escape via crafted WebAssembly module — no confirmed customer impact

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Single blog announcement provides no visibility into autonomy features, security response latency, AI-workload defense coverage, internal AI controls, or adversarial-attack readiness.

vendors.name: Akamai
vendors.wr_take: 2025 Salesforce breach was limited to third-party support ticket data with automatic deletion after 120 days. Design won by default.

cve_list.description: Identity Cloud SSRF via OAuth redirect parameter

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Akamai demonstrates foundational awareness of AI-era threats (AI bot activity, vulnerable LLM deployments) and employs ML-powered detection, but evidence lacks autonomous remediation capabilities, published response-time benchmarks, comprehensive AI workload defense (model endpoints, training data protection), formal AI governance disclosure, or research demonstrating proactive adversarial readiness.

vendors.name: Zscaler
vendors.wr_take: August 2025 Salesloft Drift supply chain breach accessed Zscaler Salesforce data via stolen OAuth tokens. Fast revocation, fast notification. Third-party integrations are the real perimeter now.

cve_list.description: ZIA DLP bypass via chunked transfer encoding evasion

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Evidence bundle consists of press releases announcing market trends and one acquisition announcement; contains no specific product feature details, technical documentation, or customer capability claims needed to assess Zscaler's autonomous remediation, AI workload defense, own-AI controls, or adversarial AI capabilities.

vendors.name: GitHub
vendors.wr_take: March 2025 tj-actions/changed-files compromise affected 23,000 repositories and leaked CI/CD secrets. The GitHub ecosystem is now a trusted supply chain vector — assume any public action can turn.

cve_list.description: Actions OIDC token leakage via workflow log output in specific configurations

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected; cannot assess vendor against AI-Era Readiness rubric without source material.

vendors.name: Atlassian
vendors.wr_take: CVE-2023-22527 in Confluence prompted exploitation attempts within days. October 2023 data breach details remain vague.

cve_list.description: Confluence RCE via malicious macro — exploited ITW in ransomware campaigns
Confluence template injection RCE — unauthenticated, mass exploitation into 2024
Information disclosure in Jira Core Data Center
Privilege escalation in Jira Core Data Center and Jira Service Management
Template injection / OGNL expression injection — unauthenticated RCE
Broken access control allowing creation of unauthorized admin accounts
Authenticated arbitrary code execution; PoC public
Command injection in multiple API endpoints — arbitrary code execution
Path traversal in Bitbucket Data Center and Server

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected for Atlassian's AI-era readiness posture; all dimensions scored NA per rubric rules requiring zero guess on absent evidence.

vendors.name: Veeam
vendors.wr_take: CVE-2024-40711 in active ransomware attacks a year after disclosure. When vulnerability velocity exceeds patch velocity, the backup system becomes the attack surface.

cve_list.description: Backup & Replication deserialization RCE — unauthenticated, exploited by ransomware groups
Backup Enterprise Manager auth bypass allowing full console access

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Veeam's ML-based Ransomware Detection Service is a traditional threat-detection product with no demonstrated AI-era readiness: alert-only posture, no published latency benchmarks, no AI workload defense, no internal AI governance disclosure, and no defense against AI-assisted attackers.

vendors.name: ServiceNow
vendors.wr_take: CVE-2025-12420 (BodySnatcher) allowed unauthenticated user impersonation bypassing MFA via virtual agents. No wild exploitation reported, but the semantic definition of vulnerability — reachable without permission — was the failure.

cve_list.description: Jelly template injection enabling unauthenticated RCE — exploited ITW
File read via path traversal in mid-server component

ai_era_grade.reasoning: ServiceNow demonstrates third-party audited AI management (ISO 42001 certification), but evidence provides only vague marketing claims on automation autonomy, contains zero indicators of AI attack surface defense capabilities, and no published data on adversarial AI readiness.

vendors.name: SAP
vendors.wr_take: CVE-2025-31324 exploited in the wild since 2023 before the April 2025 emergency patch. Recovery timelines stretched into months for affected manufacturers.

cve_list.description: NetWeaver AS ABAP OS command injection via report execution — exploited ITW
BusinessObjects information disclosure via crafted BI Launchpad request

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Evidence bundle contains only general business AI impact content (talent productivity, customer case studies, partnership announcements) with zero information on security products, autonomous remediation, detection velocity, AI attack defenses, own-AI governance, or adversarial readiness.

vendors.name: Schneider Electric
vendors.wr_take: Three incidents in 18 months. Ransomware targeting the same division repeatedly looks more like a structural pattern than bad luck.

cve_list.description: EcoStruxure Panel Server unauthenticated path traversal — config file exfiltration
Modicon M340 PLC hardcoded credentials in web server component

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No public evidence collected; cannot score any dimension without product documentation, published benchmarks, or vendor communications.

vendors.name: Siemens
vendors.wr_take: April 2024 BlackBasta breach exposed 800GB. Multiple ICS products carry critical unpatched flaws — operational systems cannot be patched on a web-scale schedule.

cve_list.description: SINEC NMS SQL injection via network management interface — unauthenticated
SIMATIC S7-1500 DoS via crafted Profinet IO — safety system impact possible

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No public evidence available — no blogs, press releases, or technical documentation collected to assess Siemens' AI-Era Readiness across any dimension.

cve_list.description: MyChart SSRF via patient portal document upload functionality

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No public evidence found for Epic Systems' AI-era security capabilities; all dimensions scored NA pending evidence collection.

vendors.name: Change Healthcare
vendors.wr_take: Feb 2024 breach disrupted US healthcare payments for weeks. Ransom paid. Second group hit the same systems weeks later. 15B transactions/year. Concentration risk was hiding in plain sight.

cve_list.description: Citrix NetScaler vuln used as initial access in February 2024 UnitedHealth breach
Citrix Bleed session token extraction — entry point in Change Healthcare compromise

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No public evidence collected; unable to assess AI-Era Readiness dimensions.

cve_list.description: PolicyCenter IDOR allowing cross-tenant policy data access

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Guidewire is an enterprise insurance software vendor (PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, BillingCenter) with internal AI for underwriting and claims, not a security product; evidence bundle contains blog post excerpts insufficient to assess any AI-Era Readiness dimension—zero evidence of autonomous threat response, detection latency, AI attack surface defense, AI governance/guardrails/disclosures, or adversarial AI readiness.

vendors.name: Proofpoint
vendors.wr_take: EchoSpoofing (Jan-June 2024) exploited misconfigured permissions to send 14 million phishing emails per day using Proofpoint's own service. The protection tool became the vector.

cve_list.description: Email Security Gateway XSS via malformed MIME boundary in quarantine interface

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Minimal vendor-published product detail in evidence bundle; only substantive content is thought leadership acknowledging agentic threat patterns in insider risk domain.

vendors.name: Barracuda Networks
vendors.wr_take: May 2023 zero-day required full hardware replacement — not patching, replacement. That is a category of incident severity most boards have no framework for.

cve_list.description: ESG appliance RCE via malformed TAR attachment — required full hardware replacement
Email Security Gateway auth bypass via crafted LDAP authentication request

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected; no public documentation found to assess Barracuda Networks against AI-Era Readiness dimensions.

cve_list.description: Alliance Gateway message manipulation via unauthenticated API endpoint

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected; cannot assess AI-Era Readiness without public documentation, product features, or published benchmarks.

vendors.name: Salesforce
vendors.wr_take: 2025 saw two waves: UNC6040 social engineering compromised 91 organizations via Data Loader spoofing, then Drift OAuth tokens hit 700+ orgs. Salesforce is now a trust boundary the product alone cannot secure.

cve_list.description: Experience Cloud guest user SOQL injection allowing data enumeration

ai_era_grade.reasoning: The evidence bundle consists of marketing materials about Salesforce's CRM products (Agentforce Grid), workforce initiatives, and general AI guidance—none of which address security operations, threat response, AI attack surface coverage, internal governance of AI systems, or adversarial AI readiness. Insufficient evidence exists to score any dimension of the security-focused AI-era readiness rubric.

vendors.name: Workday
vendors.wr_take: August 2025 social engineering attack on internal CRM and Drift OAuth exposure. Fast disclosure, clear customer guidance, no customer tenant access.

cve_list.description: HCM SSRF via integration connector URL parameter

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected; no public sources found to assess Workday's AI-era readiness across any dimension.

vendors.name: Dragos
vendors.wr_take: May 2023 extortion attempt and November 2023 AlphV claim both failed to penetrate core systems. Layered controls held. Incident response architecture works.

cve_list.description: Platform stored XSS via asset tag field in industrial asset inventory

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected for this vendor; all dimensions scored NA per rubric guidelines.

cve_list.description: IdentityIQ improper access control — HTTP access to protected static content
IdentityIQ incorrect content type XSS

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected for SailPoint; public documentation on AI-era readiness posture not found. All dimensions scored NA per scoring rules.

cve_list.description: Improper input validation in EOL OVA Connect component

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Saviynt demonstrates an IAM-centric stance on AI agent security through posture management and visibility frameworks, but evidence is limited to blog excerpts lacking product documentation. No evidence of autonomous remediation, detection-to-containment benchmarks, model endpoint protection, published AI governance policies, or adversarial defenses.

vendors.name: Commvault
vendors.wr_take: Commvault patched CVE-2025-34028 (CVSS 10) in 3 days. But active KEV exploitation and a nation-state breach via CVE-2025-3928 suggest detection lagged behind the patch velocity.

cve_list.description: Command Center path traversal RCE — pre-auth remote code execution via ZIP upload
Web Server vuln exploited by nation-state for Azure environment breach March 2025
CommServe argument injection — unauthenticated RCE via qlogin manipulation

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Evidence limited to vague reference to protecting AI workloads; no specific product features, autonomous capabilities, velocity benchmarks, internal AI controls, or adversarial readiness demonstrated across dimensions.

cve_list.description: Wiz platform security vulnerability

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No public evidence collected; all dimensions unscoreable without documentation, product claims, or benchmarks.

cve_list.description: Google Cloud security infrastructure vulnerability

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Google Cloud Security's public materials mention AI-powered capabilities, Gemini integration, task automation, and 'responsible AI' language, but lack substantive evidence on autonomous decision-making scope, published threat detection latency, AI workload defense, internal AI governance details, or adversarial AI readiness.

cve_list.description: Netskope Client static token auth allowing unauthorized enrollment and impersonation
Netskope Client config tampering via MITM

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Netskope's blog posts acknowledge AI security as a topic (governance, visibility, agentic AI) but the excerpts contain only thematic positioning without substantive product feature claims; evidence insufficient to score autonomy, velocity, own-ai posture, or adversarial readiness.

cve_list.description: PingAM Java Policy Agent path traversal — file manipulation and data exfiltration without user interaction
PingFederate Google Adapter thread exhaustion via non-200 responses

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Evidence is marketing-only with no technical depth; ForgeRock is an identity governance platform using AI internally, not a security product defending AI attack surfaces; no specifics on automation scope, latency, adversarial AI readiness, or customer-facing AI guardrails.

vendors.name: WatchTowr
vendors.wr_take: The team that finds the flaws everyone else missed. WatchTowr Labs has redefined what 'responsibly disclosed' means — Citrix Bleed, Fortinet zero-days, Ivanti EPMM. They don't build walls; they hunt the holes.

ai_era_grade.reasoning: Single brief blog excerpt with only velocity claim (hours→minutes) being specific and measurable; autonomy and own-AI inferred from vague 'AI-driven rapid reaction' language without product feature details; zero evidence for AI surface coverage or adversarial readiness.

vendors.name: Vanta
vendors.wr_take: Market leader in compliance automation. The March 2025 cross-customer data exposure — caused by a product code change, not an attacker — was the reminder that compliance platforms are still humans with keyboards. Affected ~4% of customers.

cve_list.description: Cross-customer data exposure: code change allowed sensitive employee data and MFA settings to bleed across customer accounts

ai_era_grade.reasoning: No evidence collected; all dimensions scored NA per rubric requirement to avoid speculation without supporting documentation.
